chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q1_analysis: The authors provide a link to the reproducibility package on Zenodo (https://zenodo.org/doi/10.5281/zenodo.10992678), which includes the implementation of SYREP and the experimental data.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q2_analysis: The evaluation uses simulations, as it is not feasible to test all possible failure scenarios in a physical network. The authors use the CUDD backend of the omega Python library for BDD-based synthesis.
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q3_analysis: A formal fault model is defined in Section II, which specifies the types of failures that the system cannot handle. The paper has a clear and explicit section detailing the 'Fault Model' stating precise assumptions (e.g., asynchronous network, up to f Byzantine nodes).
chain_of_thought_evaluation.Q4_analysis: The authors provide a rigorous analysis of the performance trade-offs in Section V, including CPU time and scalability. They also discuss the limitations of their approach for larger sets of failed links.

major_weakness: The evaluation relies entirely on standard software simulations without physical hardware or large-scale deployment, which may limit the generalizability of the results.
